Data Science & Business Analytics Trends
  • Home
  • Project Overview
  • Data Preparation
  • Exploratory Analysis
  • Skill Gap Analysis
  • Predictive Modeling
  • Career Strategy

On this page

  • Keeping Necessary Columns
  • Handling Missing Values
    • Numerical Fields - Filled with Median
    • Columns with >50% Missing - Dropped
    • Categorical Fields - Filled with “Unknown”
  • Remove Duplicates
  • Save as CSV

Data Preparation

import pandas as pd
print("working")
working
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

# Start a Spark session
spark = SparkSession.builder.config("spark.driver.host", "localhost").appName("JobPostingsAnalysis").getOrCreate()
spark.catalog.clearCache()

# Load the CSV file into a Spark DataFrame
df = spark.read.option("header", "true").option("inferSchema", "true").option(
    "multiLine", "true").option("escape", "\"").csv("data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

# Register the DataFrame as a temporary SQL view
df.createOrReplaceTempView("job_postings")

# Show Schema and Sample Data
print("---This is Diagnostic check, No need to print it in the final doc---")

# comment the lines below when rendering the submission
df.printSchema()
df.show(5)
WARNING: Using incubator modules: jdk.incubator.vector
Using Spark's default log4j profile: org/apache/spark/log4j2-defaults.properties
Setting default log level to "WARN".
To adjust logging level use sc.setLogLevel(newLevel). For SparkR, use setLogLevel(newLevel).
26/04/09 00:30:05 WARN NativeCodeLoader: Unable to load native-hadoop library for your platform... using builtin-java classes where applicable

[Stage 1:>                                                          (0 + 1) / 1]

                                                                                
26/04/09 00:30:18 WARN SparkStringUtils: Truncated the string representation of a plan since it was too large. This behavior can be adjusted by setting 'spark.sql.debug.maxToStringFields'.

 |-- REMOTE_TYPE: integer (nullable = true)
 |-- REMOTE_TYPE_NAME: string (nullable = true)
 |-- ORIGINAL_PAY_PERIOD: string (nullable = true)
 |-- SALARY_TO: integer (nullable = true)
 |-- SALARY_FROM: integer (nullable = true)

Information Techn...|15-0000|Computer and Math...|15-2000|Mathematical Scie...|15-2050|Data Scientists|15-2051|Data Scientists|             NULL|                  NULL|          99|Unclassified Indu...|         999|Unclassified Indu...|        9999|Unclassified Indu...|       99999|Unclassified Indu...|      999999|Unclassified Indu...|
+--------------------+-----------------+----------------------+----------+--------+---------+--------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+-----------+-------------------+--------------------+--------------------+---------------+----------------+--------+--------------------+-----------+-------------------+----------------+---------------------+-------------+-------------------+-------------+------------------+---------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+-------------+------+-----------+----------------+-------------------+---------+-----------+--------------------+--------------------+-------------+------+--------------+-----+--------------------+-----+----------+---------------+--------------------+---------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+------+--------------------+------+--------------------+------+--------------------+------+--------------------+------+--------------------+------------------+-------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+-----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+----------+--------------------+----------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+----------+--------------------+----------+--------------------+----------+---------------+----------+---------------+---------------+--------------------+--------------+--------------------+--------------------------+-------------------------------+--------------------+-------------------------+-----------------------------+----------------------------------+-----------------+----------------------+-----------------------+----------------------------+------------------+-----------------------+-------+--------------------+-------+--------------------+-------+---------------+-------+---------------+-----------------+----------------------+------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+
only showing top 5 rows

Keeping Necessary Columns

The following columns were chosen as they provide relevant information for analysis. NIACS, ONET, CIP, SOC, and LOT categories all had multiple different columns - only the latest were kept in order to reflect the current classifications. Cutting down the amount of columns and data set overall helps to make a cleaner data set to work with with less clutter.

from pyspark.sql.functions import col, monotonically_increasing_id

clean_df = df.select(
    col("ID"),
    col("POSTED"),
    col("COMPANY_NAME"),
    col("COMPANY_RAW"),
    col("COMPANY_IS_STAFFING"),
    col("EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME"),
    col("MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME"),
    col("MAX_EDULEVELS_NAME"),
    col("EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME"),
    col("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE"), 
    col("MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE"), 
    col("SALARY"),
    col("SALARY_TO"),
    col("SALARY_FROM"),
    col("REMOTE_TYPE_NAME"), 
    col("LOCATION"),
    col("CITY_NAME"),
    col("COUNTY_NAME"),
    col("MSA_NAME"),
    col("STATE_NAME"),
    col("NAICS6"),
    col("NAICS6_NAME"),
    col("TITLE_NAME"),
    col("TITLE_CLEAN"),
    col("SKILLS_NAME"),
    col("SPECIALIZED_SKILLS_NAME"),
    col("CERTIFICATIONS_NAME"),
    col("COMMON_SKILLS_NAME"),
    col("SOFTWARE_SKILLS_NAME"),
    col("ONET_NAME"),
    col("CIP6_NAME"),
    col("SOC_5_NAME").alias("SOC_NAME"),
    col("SOC_5").alias("SOC_CODE"),
    col("LOT_V6_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION_NAME").alias("SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION"),
    col("LOT_V6_OCCUPATION_NAME").alias("OCCUPATION"),
    col("LOT_V6_OCCUPATION_GROUP_NAME").alias("0CCUPATION_GROUP"),
    col("LOT_V6_CAREER_AREA_NAME").alias("CAREER_AREA"),
    col("NAICS_2022_6"),
    col("NAICS_2022_6_NAME")
)

clean_df.show(10)
+--------------------+--------+--------------------+--------------------+-------------------+---------------------+-------------------+------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+------+---------+-----------+----------------+--------------------+------------------+--------------------+--------------------+----------+------+--------------------+-------------------+--------------------+--------------------+-----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+---------------+--------+----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+
|                  ID|  POSTED|        COMPANY_NAME|         COMPANY_RAW|COMPANY_IS_STAFFING|EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME| MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME|MAX_EDULEVELS_NAME|EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME|MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE|MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE|SALARY|SALARY_TO|SALARY_FROM|REMOTE_TYPE_NAME|            LOCATION|         CITY_NAME|         COUNTY_NAME|            MSA_NAME|STATE_NAME|NAICS6|         NAICS6_NAME|         TITLE_NAME|         TITLE_CLEAN|         SKILLS_NAME|SPECIALIZED_SKILLS_NAME| CERTIFICATIONS_NAME|  COMMON_SKILLS_NAME|SOFTWARE_SKILLS_NAME|           ONET_NAME|           CIP6_NAME|       SOC_NAME|SOC_CODE|SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION|          OCCUPATION|    0CCUPATION_GROUP|         CAREER_AREA|NAICS_2022_6|   NAICS_2022_6_NAME|
+--------------------+--------+--------------------+--------------------+-------------------+---------------------+-------------------+------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+------+---------+-----------+----------------+--------------------+------------------+--------------------+--------------------+----------+------+--------------------+-------------------+--------------------+--------------------+-----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+---------------+--------+----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+

|85318b12b3331fa49...|6/2/2024|            Sedgwick|            Sedgwick|              false| [\n  "Bachelor's ...|  Bachelor's degree|              NULL|Full-time (> 32 h...|                   5|                NULL|  NULL|     NULL|       NULL|          [None]|{\n  "lat": 32.77...|        Dallas, TX|          Dallas, TX|Dallas-Fort Worth...|     Texas|524291|    Claims Adjusting|      Data Analysts|        data analyst|[\n  "Management"...|   [\n  "Exception R...|[\n  "Security Cl...|[\n  "Management"...|[\n  "Microsoft O...|Business Intellig...|                  []|Data Scientists| 15-2051|          Data Analyst|Data / Data Minin...|Data Analysis and...|Information Techn...|      524291|    Claims Adjusting|
|1b5c3941e54a1889e...|6/2/2024|         Wells Fargo|         Wells Fargo|              false| [\n  "No Educatio...|No Education Listed|              NULL|Full-time (> 32 h...|                   3|                NULL|  NULL|     NULL|       NULL|          [None]|{\n  "lat": 33.44...|       Phoenix, AZ|        Maricopa, AZ|Phoenix-Mesa-Chan...|   Arizona|522110|  Commercial Banking|Management Analysts|sr lead data mgmt...|[\n  "Exit Strate...|   [\n  "Exit Strate...|                  []|[\n  "Reliability...|[\n  "SAS (Softwa...|Business Intellig...|                  []|Data Scientists| 15-2051|          Data Analyst|Data / Data Minin...|Data Analysis and...|Information Techn...|      522110|  Commercial Banking|
|cb5ca25f02bdf25c1...|6/2/2024|        Unclassified|               LH/GM|              false| [\n  "No Educatio...|No Education Listed|              NULL|Part-time / full-...|                NULL|                NULL| 92500|   150000|      35000|          [None]|{\n  "lat": 37.63...|       Modesto, CA|      Stanislaus, CA|         Modesto, CA|California|999999|Unclassified Indu...|       Unclassified|comisiones de por...|                  []|                     []|                  []|                  []|                  []|Business Intellig...|                  []|Data Scientists| 15-2051|  Oracle Consultant...|Business Intellig...|Business Intellig...|Information Techn...|      999999|Unclassified Indu...|
|35a6cd2183d9fb270...|6/2/2024|  Lumen Technologies|               Lumen|              false| [\n  "Bachelor's ...|  Bachelor's degree|              NULL|Full-time (> 32 h...|                NULL|                NULL|110155|   125890|      94420|          Remote|{\n  "lat": 0,\n ...|[Unknown City], AR|[Unknown county], AR|                NULL|  Arkansas|517810|All Other Telecom...| Lead Data Analysts|sr lead data analyst|[\n  "Power BI",\...|   [\n  "Power BI",\...|                  []|[\n  "Presentatio...|[\n  "Power BI",\...|Business Intellig...|[\n  "Business Ad...|Data Scientists| 15-2051|          Data Analyst|Data / Data Minin...|Data Analysis and...|Information Techn...|      517810|All Other Telecom...|
|06de8d192f30b1d8d...|6/2/2024|Semiconductor Com...|Semiconductor Com...|              false| [\n  "Bachelor's ...|  Bachelor's degree|              NULL|Full-time (> 32 h...|                NULL|                NULL|  NULL|     NULL|       NULL|          [None]|{\n  "lat": 33.49...|    Scottsdale, AZ|        Maricopa, AZ|Phoenix-Mesa-Chan...|   Arizona|334413|Semiconductor and...|   IT Data Analysts| talent data analyst|[\n  "Interactive...|   [\n  "Interactive...|                  []|[\n  "Analytical ...|[\n  "Interactive...|Business Intellig...|                  []|Data Scientists| 15-2051|          Data Analyst|Data / Data Minin...|Data Analysis and...|Information Techn...|      334413|Semiconductor and...|
|3d589c9d84677ca94...|6/2/2024|            Sedgwick|            Sedgwick|              false| [\n  "Bachelor's ...|  Bachelor's degree|              NULL|Full-time (> 32 h...|                   5|                NULL|  NULL|     NULL|       NULL|          [None]|{\n  "lat": 39.75...|        Dayton, OH|      Montgomery, OH|Dayton-Kettering, OH|      Ohio|524291|    Claims Adjusting|      Data Analysts|        data analyst|[\n  "Management"...|   [\n  "Exception R...|[\n  "Security Cl...|[\n  "Management"...|[\n  "Microsoft O...|Business Intellig...|                  []|Data Scientists| 15-2051|          Data Analyst|Data / Data Minin...|Data Analysis and...|Information Techn...|      524291|    Claims Adjusting|

|229620073766234e8...|6/2/2024|  Dassault SystÃ¨mes|    Dassault Systmes|              false| [\n  "Bachelor's ...|  Bachelor's degree|   Master's degree|Full-time (> 32 h...|                   2|                   2| 92962|   106424|      79500|          [None]|{\n  "lat": 40.75...|      New York, NY|        New York, NY|New York-Newark-J...|  New York|541511|Custom Computer P...| Marketing Analysts|sr marketing analyst|[\n  "Salesforce"...|   [\n  "Salesforce"...|                  []|[\n  "Prioritizat...|[\n  "Salesforce"...|Business Intellig...|[\n  "Business/Co...|Data Scientists| 15-2051|          Data Analyst|Data / Data Minin...|Data Analysis and...|Information Techn...|      541511|Custom Computer P...|
+--------------------+--------+--------------------+--------------------+-------------------+---------------------+-------------------+------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+------+---------+-----------+----------------+--------------------+------------------+--------------------+--------------------+----------+------+--------------------+-------------------+--------------------+--------------------+-----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+---------------+--------+----------------------+--------------------+--------------------+--------------------+------------+--------------------+
only showing top 10 rows

Handling Missing Values

Numerical Fields - Filled with Median

import os
from pyspark.sql. functions import regexp_replace

clean_df = clean_df.withColumn ("SALARY_FROM",col("SALARY_FROM").cast("float")) \
    .withColumn("SALARY_TO", col("SALARY_TO").cast("float")) \
    .withColumn("SALARY", col("SALARY").cast("float")) \
    .withColumn("MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE", col("MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE").cast("float"))

def compute_median (sdf, col_name):
    q = sdf.approxQuantile(col_name, [0.5], 0.01)
    return q[0] if q else None

median_from = compute_median(clean_df, "SALARY_FROM")
median_to = compute_median(clean_df, "SALARY_TO")
median_salary = compute_median(clean_df, "SALARY")

print("Medians:", median_from, median_to, median_salary)

clean_df = clean_df.fillna({
    "SALARY_FROM": median_from,
    "SALARY_TO": median_to
})

clean_df = clean_df.withColumn("AVERAGE_SALARY", (col("SALARY_FROM") + col("SALARY_TO")) / 2)

clean_df = clean_df.withColumn(
    "EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME",
    regexp_replace(col("EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME"), r"[\n\r]","")
)

clean_df.show(10)
clean_pd = clean_df.toPandas()

[Stage 4:>                                                          (0 + 1) / 1]

                                                                                

[Stage 5:>                                                          (0 + 1) / 1]

                                                                                

[Stage 6:>                                                          (0 + 1) / 1]

only showing top 10 rows

[Stage 8:>                                                          (0 + 1) / 1]

Columns with >50% Missing - Dropped

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import missingno as msno

msno.heatmap(clean_pd)
plt.title("Missing Values Heatmap")
plt.show()

clean_pd.dropna(thresh=len(clean_pd) * 0.5, axis=1, inplace=True)

Categorical Fields - Filled with “Unknown”

categorical_cols = clean_pd.select_dtypes(include=["object", "category", "string"]).columns.tolist()
numerical_cols = clean_pd.select_dtypes(include=["number"]).columns.tolist()

print("Categorical:", categorical_cols)
print("Numerical:", numerical_cols)
Categorical: ['ID', 'POSTED', 'COMPANY_NAME', 'COMPANY_RAW', 'COMPANY_IS_STAFFING', 'EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME', 'MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME', 'EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME', 'REMOTE_TYPE_NAME', 'LOCATION', 'CITY_NAME', 'COUNTY_NAME', 'MSA_NAME', 'STATE_NAME', 'NAICS6_NAME', 'TITLE_NAME', 'TITLE_CLEAN', 'SKILLS_NAME', 'SPECIALIZED_SKILLS_NAME', 'CERTIFICATIONS_NAME', 'COMMON_SKILLS_NAME', 'SOFTWARE_SKILLS_NAME', 'ONET_NAME', 'CIP6_NAME', 'SOC_NAME', 'SOC_CODE', 'SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION', 'OCCUPATION', '0CCUPATION_GROUP', 'CAREER_AREA', 'NAICS_2022_6_NAME']
Numerical: ['MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE', 'SALARY_TO', 'SALARY_FROM', 'NAICS6', 'NAICS_2022_6', 'AVERAGE_SALARY']
cols = ['ID','COMPANY_NAME', 'COMPANY_RAW', 'COMPANY_IS_STAFFING', 'EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME', 'MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME', 'EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME', 'MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE','REMOTE_TYPE_NAME', 'CITY_NAME', 'COUNTY_NAME', 'MSA_NAME', 'STATE_NAME','NAICS6','NAICS6_NAME', 'TITLE_NAME', 'TITLE_CLEAN', 'SKILLS_NAME', 'SPECIALIZED_SKILLS_NAME', 'CERTIFICATIONS_NAME', 'COMMON_SKILLS_NAME', 'SOFTWARE_SKILLS_NAME', 'ONET_NAME', 'CIP6_NAME', 'SOC_NAME', 'SOC_CODE', 'SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION', 'OCCUPATION', '0CCUPATION_GROUP', 'CAREER_AREA', 'NAICS_2022_6','NAICS_2022_6_NAME', 'LOCATION', 'POSTED']

clean_pd[cols] = clean_pd[cols].fillna("Unknown")

clean_pd.isnull().sum()
clean_pd.head(10)
ID POSTED COMPANY_NAME COMPANY_RAW COMPANY_IS_STAFFING EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE SALARY_TO ... CIP6_NAME SOC_NAME SOC_CODE SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION OCCUPATION 0CCUPATION_GROUP CAREER_AREA NAICS_2022_6 NAICS_2022_6_NAME AVERAGE_SALARY

10 rows × 37 columns

Remove Duplicates

This step helps to ensure that there is no duplication in the data set and only each job posting is counted once.

clean_pd = clean_pd.drop_duplicates(subset=["TITLE_NAME", "COMPANY_NAME", "LOCATION", "POSTED"], keep="first")

Save as CSV

clean_pd.to_csv("data/clean_job_postings.csv", index=False)
